title: Garage/Mudroom Storage and Organizer
summary: We needed a broom closet and storage in our garage.
description: We needed a broom closet and storage in our garage. Bench area to put on shoes or boots, with shoe/boot storage below. We live in a northern climate and use our garage as a refrigerator in the winter time, so the shelves are to store items on, and drawers to install misc. outdoor items.

steps.title: Construct base
steps.body: The base is constructed of (3) 2x4's 8' long cut as shown. I had to taper my base because the garage floor slopes

steps.title: Construct the closet box
steps.body: Construct the closet with dimensions as shown. Use pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws to attached the top and bottom the the side panels. Locate the pocket holes on the top and bottom so that they are concealed

steps.title: Attach wall nailers
steps.body: Attach wall nailers at the the top and bottom of the box as shown. Also add one near the middle to keep the tall box square and straight.

steps.title: Add the face frame
steps.body: Construct a face frame from 1x2's as shown. Join the frame with pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and brad nail and glue the face frame to the closet box

steps.title: Add a door
steps.body: I purchased an old three panel door that was 3/4" x 6'-8" from a recycling store. I had to trim it slightly to fit the height. If you cannot find a door you can construct one to fit the face frame opening that is 13" wide x 78 1/2" with 1x8's joined together and cut to correct width.

steps.title: Construct drawer boxes
steps.body: Construct drawer boxes with dimensions as shown. Add 3/4" x 3/4" blocking at the bottom of each box by glueing and nailing. These are the guides for the drawer. Add 3/4" x 1/2" blocking at the top of each box by glueing and nailing. These are the guides to keep the drawers level as they are pulled out. Screw the boxes together. I used (3) 1 1/4" pocket hole screws front and back of the bottom box into the top box.

steps.title: Construct the shelf box
steps.body: Construct the shelf box with dimensions as shown. Join with pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws in the top and bottom pieces. I drilled shelf pin holes down each side of the sides of the plywood box so that we could have adjustable shelves. Screw the shelf box to the drawer box with (3) 1 1/4" pocket hole screws front and back from the top drawer box into the bottom of the shelf box.

steps.title: Construct the drawers
steps.body: Cut four side pieces and 4 front/back pieces with dimensions as shown. Cut rabbit into bottom edge of all pieces.

steps.title: Add the plywood bottom
steps.body: Install the plywood bottom to the drawer parts

steps.title: Final assembly of drawers
steps.body: Install the other side and back/front to the drawer box. Assemble all with pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Locate the screws so they are not visible when the drawers are completed.

steps.title: Add drawer front to boxes
steps.body: Add a drawer front to the drawer boxes with dimensions as shown. Add your choice of hardware

steps.title: Construct face frame/install drawer
steps.body: Construct face frame for shelves and drawers as shown using 1x2 material. Join the face frame with pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and glue and nail to boxes

steps.title: Install the closet box
steps.body: Install the closet box to the base with cabinet screws utilizing the top bottom and middle nailer.

steps.title: Install the shelf box
steps.body: Install the shelf box utilizing cabinet screws into the nailers. Also screw the face frames together utilizing cabinet screws.

steps.title: Construct the shoe storage boxes
steps.body: Construct the shoe storage box as shown. Join with pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Install the screws so that they will not be visible.

steps.title: Install the shoe boxes
steps.body: Install the shoe boxes as shown. Join them together with (3) 1 1/4" pocket hole screws front and box from one box into the other. Also screw the boxes to the base with (2) 1 1/4" pocket hole screw front and back of the box

steps.title: Add face frame and bench top
steps.body: Add face frame for the shoe boxes of 1x2 material. Join the face frame with pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the face frame to the boxes with glue and nails. Install a bench top. The dimensions for the upper portion are based on using a piece of 3/4" material for the bench. I used a reclaim board that ended up 1 1/2" thick. Adjust the upper box accordingly based on your use of material. Screw the bench to to the boxes with 1 1/4" screws thru the top of the box into the bench.

steps.title: Construct the upper boxes
steps.body: Construct the upper boxes with dimensions as shown.

steps.title: Add nailer
steps.body: Add nailer to the back of the upper box.

steps.title: Join the two boxes
steps.body: Join the two boxes. Use screws similar to the bottom boxes.

steps.title: Add side supports
steps.body: Add side supports to the boxes as shown. Glue and nail to the boxes

steps.title: Install the top box assembly
steps.body: Install the top box assembly utilizing cabinet screws into the nailer and pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws into the bench top

steps.title: Add the face frame
steps.body: Construct a face frame utilizing pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Attache to the upper assembly with glue and nails. Anchor the face frame to the adjoining shelf unit with cabinet screws thru the side of the face frame.

steps.title: Finish the unit
steps.body: Finish the unit by painting or stain.....your choice. I also lined the bottom shoe storage boxes with carpet squares cut to size and glued in place on the sides, back and bottom of each box.

steps.title: Seat area
steps.body: Install coat hangers on the rear area above the seat. I used left over pieces of plywood, cut a rabbit in one edge and glued and nailed the pieces to the wall above the seat.....looks similar to ship-lap. You can do something similar, or install bead board, or just paint the wall.
